steps.action: Open README.md on GitHub via agent-browser
steps.expected: README renders correctly with proper Markdown formatting
steps.observed: README renders correctly - title, badges, and content visible

steps.expected: Opening paragraph clearly states UI.md value proposition
steps.observed: Opening paragraph: 'UI.md is a behavioral contract that keeps AI agents, designers, and developers in sync.' (within 50 words)

evidence.network: GitHub page loaded successfully, all resources returned HTTP 200/302

steps.expected: README explicitly names coordination problem with concrete examples
steps.observed: README identifies 'coordination problem' and lists 3 concrete failure modes: Agents produce screens that don't connect, State handling becomes inconsistent, Data contracts drift apart

evidence.quote: When multiple AI agents work on the same UI, they don't know when their outputs need to fit together. Without a shared contract: Agents produce screens that don't connect — navigation graphs silently diverge; State handling becomes inconsistent — one agent models loading states, another doesn't; Data contracts drift apart — the 'user' entity has different fields in different agents' outputs

steps.expected: Quick-start section with ≤5 numbered steps and code snippets
steps.observed: Quick Start section has exactly 5 steps (Step 1: Explore a Sample, Step 2: Create Your First UI.md, Step 3: Add the Machine-Readable Appendix, Step 4: Install the Linter, Step 5: Validate)

steps.action: Verify each step has code/markdown snippets
steps.expected: Each step includes actionable code/markdown snippets
steps.observed: Each step contains markdown examples (e.g., UI.md skeleton, YAML appendix) or CLI commands (npm install, node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js)

evidence.lineReferences: Lines 23-100 in README.md contain Quick Start section with 5 numbered steps

steps.action: Verify comparison section exists with ≥3 alternatives
steps.observed: Comparison table at line 104 lists 4 alternatives: DESIGN.md, PRD/SPEC.md, Storybook, API.md

steps.action: Verify each alternative's limitation is named
steps.expected: Each alternative's limitation is named and UI.md differentiation is clear
steps.observed: Table columns show: Document, What It Covers, UI.md Differentiation for each alternative

steps.expected: README references samples/ directory or external adoption
steps.observed: README at line 120 states 'See real examples in samples/'

steps.expected: At least one example shows real application flow
steps.observed: README lists three samples with descriptions of non-trivial flows: kanban (drag-drop, filters, real-time states), mobile-onboarding (permissions, progress persistence), cli-dashboard (keyboard navigation, streaming output)

steps.expected: All links resolve to valid URLs
steps.observed: GitHub main: 200, MIT badge: 200, MIT license (redirect): 301, SPEC.md: 302, CONTRIBUTING.md: 302, AGENTS.md: 302

steps.expected: All badges render with correct status
steps.observed: MIT license badge and Version badge visible in screenshots

evidence.linkValidation: curl checks confirmed HTTP 200/301/302 for all tested links (redirects are expected for GitHub)

steps.action: Verify installation section exists with prerequisites
steps.expected: Installation section includes prerequisite list, install command, usage example
steps.observed: Linter Installation and Usage section (lines 130-165) includes: Prerequisites (Node.js ≥18), Installation commands (git clone, npm install), Usage examples (node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js), Exit codes table

steps.action: Verify CONTRIBUTING link present in README
steps.expected: CONTRIBUTING link present with brief description
steps.observed: Contributing section at line 181 states 'We welcome contributions! See CONTRIBUTING.md for:' followed by topics covered

summary: Tested 8 assertions (VAL-README-001 through VAL-README-008): all 8 passed. README.md renders correctly on GitHub, clearly articulates the coordination problem UI.md solves, provides a 5-step quick-start guide, compares with 4 alternatives, references non-trivial sample applications, has valid badges and links, includes complete installation/usage instructions, and provides clear contributing guidance.
